title: Youre Not Going To Get Me To Say Anythin

jokes.text: I didn't know a spite store was gonna be this much work.
jokes.setup: Larry is renovating a coffee shop to spite Mocha Joe
jokes.punchline: He's surprised his petty revenge requires actual business effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - his petty motivations clash with reality in a character-specific way

jokes.text: When you're home, making love to your fuckin' lady, you're on top of her, and you... and you pumpin' her, you say, 'I'm comin', I'm comin', I'm comin',' you don't get the fuck up and go in the kitchen and make a goddamn ham sandwich, do you?
jokes.setup: Leon takes the phone to deal with a late electrician
jokes.punchline: Escalates to an absurd sexual analogy about following through on commitments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon moment - takes a simple concept and escalates it to hilariously inappropriate extremes with perfect character voice

jokes.text: You need a motherfucker like me around here.
jokes.setup: Leon just terrorized the electrician with his sexual analogy
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-factly presents himself as essential management
jokes.explanation: Leon's confidence after his unhinged rant is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Don't serve those, those dry-ass scones. Those shits make my throat hurt.
jokes.setup: Leon is being put in charge of the coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Immediately starts giving business advice about scones in his crude style
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon - takes charge immediately with his own crude priorities

jokes.text: Apparently, there was some lack of oxygen to the brain, and... legally speaking, she's now non compos mentis.
jokes.setup: Someone choked on Larry's scones
jokes.punchline: Larry casually describes serious brain damage with legal terminology
jokes.explanation: Dark Larry humor - treats serious injury as a legal technicality that benefits him

jokes.text: So that scone got you out of a lot of trouble. Eh, I like 'em. They're good. I'm gonna keep 'em.
jokes.setup: The scone caused brain damage but eliminated a lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Larry decides to keep serving the dangerous scones because they worked out for him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry selfishness - someone's brain damage is just a business advantage to him

jokes.text: Hey, fuck you, Vanilla Joe. Kiss my ass.
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe arrives to taunt them
jokes.punchline: Leon calls him 'Vanilla Joe' as an insult, flipping the coffee flavor hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Clever name-flip that works as both insult and coffee reference

jokes.text: You don't have the beans.
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe is taunting Larry's coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Double meaning - literally coffee beans, figuratively courage/ability
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that sets up the beans quest, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: He's fuckin' right. You got to have the fuckin' beans to have the best coffee.
jokes.setup: They taste Mocha Joe's superior coffee
jokes.punchline: Leon immediately agrees with their enemy, missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Leon's pragmatic response to being insulted fits his character

jokes.text: So you go for the napkin on the lap immediately. Have you ever caught anything on that napkin? No.
jokes.setup: Discussion about proper napkin placement at restaurants
jokes.punchline: The napkin never actually catches anything despite the effort
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry observation about social conventions that don't actually work

jokes.text: By the way, the dignity that you lose by tucking it in, I'd rather get new shirts and new ties.
jokes.setup: Discussing tucking napkin into collar like a bib
jokes.punchline: Larry would rather buy new clothes than look undignified
jokes.explanation: Larry's vanity and social anxiety about appearances is perfectly on brand

jokes.text: Who tucks in? Al Capone, you know. Yeah, no one's gonna question him.
jokes.setup: Continuing napkin tucking discussion
jokes.punchline: Only Al Capone could get away with looking silly because of his criminal reputation
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation connecting gangster intimidation to restaurant etiquette

jokes.text: Look, you're just not gonna get me to say anything bad about Mickey.
jokes.setup: Larry starts complaining about Mickey's wedding plans
jokes.punchline: Jeff preemptively refuses to criticize Mickey, knowing Larry's game
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of Jeff's loyalty that becomes funnier as the episode progresses

jokes.text: You're not invited to the wedding. Nah, fuck the wedding. I'm going with you. Tagging along with you.
jokes.setup: Larry and Jeff plan to split a plane to Cabo
jokes.punchline: Leon casually reveals he's not invited but plans to crash anyway
jokes.explanation: Leon's shameless approach to wedding crashing fits his bold character perfectly

jokes.text: Consider me your luggage. Bring me on the fuckin' plane, put a seatbelt around me, and I won't say shit.
jokes.setup: Leon wants to come on the plane without paying
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to inanimate luggage to justify not paying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon logic - creates absurd rules to get what he wants while sounding reasonable

jokes.text: By the way, luggage doesn't eat. Or talk. Or chip in.
jokes.setup: Leon compares himself to luggage
jokes.punchline: Larry points out the logical flaws in Leon's luggage analogy
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that deflates Leon's absurd logic with simple facts

jokes.text: Would you mind throwing out my gum? It's all wrapped up in paper. It's like throwing out paper.
jokes.setup: Larry asks a stranger to throw out his gum
jokes.punchline: Minimizes the grossness by focusing on the wrapper instead of contents
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social manipulation - reframes gross request as reasonable favor

jokes.text: Pocket? You know what happens? You forget, then it goes in the wash and it ruins the pants.
jokes.setup: Person suggests putting gum in pocket instead
jokes.punchline: Larry has a detailed nightmare scenario about pocket gum
jokes.explanation: Larry's obsession with worst-case scenarios to justify weird behavior

jokes.text: Give me one reason why you wouldn't do it. You don't have one.
jokes.setup: Larry has explained his gum disposal logic
jokes.punchline: Challenges the person to find fault with his obviously unreasonable request
jokes.explanation: Larry's confidence in his flawed logic is perfectly in character

jokes.text: People always have a great time in sushi bars 'cause they don't have to look at each other.
jokes.setup: Larry and date are enjoying sitting at sushi bar
jokes.punchline: Larry attributes the good time to avoiding eye contact rather than the food or atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry observation that reframes romantic dining as antisocial convenience

jokes.text: What could be worse than having to sit across the table... staring at somebody?
jokes.setup: Continuing his anti-eye contact philosophy
jokes.punchline: Treats looking at someone as torture rather than intimacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry dating philosophy - romance is an ordeal to be avoided

jokes.text: Usually, that's like, a fifth, sixth, seventh date kind of thing.
jokes.setup: Larry invites her to a wedding in Mexico after one date
jokes.punchline: She points out the absurd timeline escalation
jokes.explanation: Standard dating observation that sets up Larry's social awkwardness

jokes.text: You would sleep on the couch. So... Oh, would I?
jokes.setup: Larry offers to bring her to Mexico but mentions sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Donna's sarcastic response highlights his presumptuous offer
jokes.explanation: Good interplay showing Larry's obliviousness to how insulting his offer sounds

jokes.text: I mean, if you like sunsets.
jokes.setup: She shows a beautiful sunset photo
jokes.punchline: Larry acts like appreciating sunsets is a weird personal quirk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry contrarianism - treats universal beauty as niche interest

jokes.text: That's you? Really? What did you have... Some, uh, was it some kind of medical thing?
jokes.setup: Donna shows photo where she was much thinner
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked reaction and inappropriate medical questions
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy from Larry's inability to hide his shock at her weight change

jokes.text: It's like, if you saw a picture of me with hair a year ago, and then you met me and I was bald, you'd be disturbed, too.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to explain why her weight change is disturbing
jokes.punchline: Uses his own baldness as analogy, accidentally insulting both of them
jokes.explanation: Larry tries to be diplomatic but makes it worse by comparing her weight gain to his baldness

jokes.text: What is better than dessert? Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Larry is disturbed by her past thinness and worried about weight gain
jokes.punchline: She immediately demolishes a large dessert with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - her eating confirms Larry's worst fears about her weight trajectory

jokes.text: I looked at that picture, I almost fainted. It's like she ate herself.
jokes.setup: Larry describes Donna's weight change to Leon
jokes.punchline: Harsh visual metaphor about her eating her former self
jokes.explanation: Dark but vivid Larry imagery that captures his shock at the transformation

jokes.text: You know what the fuck that means? She's a fuckin' yo-yo. All signs point to her yo-ing up.
jokes.setup: Leon analyzes Donna's weight fluctuation pattern
jokes.punchline: Uses yo-yo metaphor and creates the verb 'yo-ing' for weight cycling
jokes.explanation: Leon's crude analysis creates a memorable metaphor that drives the episode's B-story

jokes.text: I got to ask her her weight now? No way I'm asking her her weight. Impossible.
jokes.setup: Private plane requires passenger weights for safety
jokes.punchline: Larry realizes he has to ask the woman he just discussed weight with for her weight
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for escalating awkwardness - his worst-case scenario becomes necessary

jokes.text: If that pilot needs my weight to fly that plane safely, he need another fuckin' plane.
jokes.setup: Larry explains weight requirements for flight safety
jokes.punchline: Leon suggests getting a different plane rather than revealing his weight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon logic - rather than compromise privacy, just get different transportation

jokes.text: Suppose I took your tray like this. What are you gonna do then? You fucked up now!
jokes.setup: Larry demonstrates how hard it is to stop determined eaters
jokes.punchline: Takes Leon's food to prove point, Leon gets genuinely angry
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that backfires - Larry's demonstration becomes real conflict

jokes.text: Now I got to eat my shit with a spoon.
jokes.setup: Larry took Leon's fork during his demonstration
jokes.punchline: Leon is stuck with the wrong utensil, proving Larry's point about determination
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the physical bit - Leon adapts rather than gives up eating

jokes.text: You don't invite a divorced couple to a wedding. It's a very combustible situation.
jokes.setup: Larry encounters his ex-wife at the wedding venue
jokes.punchline: Describes divorced couples as explosive devices at weddings
jokes.explanation: Good Larry theory about social dynamics with military metaphor

jokes.text: People resent you if you don't chip in. There's the resentment, right there.
jokes.setup: Cheryl resists paying for the plane
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately identifies her resistance as the resentment he predicted
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy where Larry's prediction comes true in real time, proving his social theories

jokes.text: One date? And you're bringing her on a trip? When you're complaining that divorced couples shouldn't come when you're bringing a virtual stranger?
jokes.setup: Larry criticizes divorced couples at weddings
jokes.punchline: Cheryl points out his hypocrisy bringing a first date
jokes.explanation: Good reality check exposing Larry's double standards about wedding appropriateness

jokes.text: You know who else doesn't know how much I weigh? My doctor.
jokes.setup: Jeff refuses to reveal his weight for the plane
jokes.punchline: Claims even his doctor doesn't know, which is medically absurd
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation of weight secrecy to impossible medical scenario

jokes.text: I'd rather be dead in the Sea of Cortez than have you know what I weigh.
jokes.setup: Jeff's extreme secrecy about his weight
jokes.punchline: Would literally prefer death to weight disclosure
jokes.explanation: Extreme hyperbole that perfectly captures vanity about weight, with specific geographic reference

jokes.text: No, the captain's for a boat. He's the pilot. Well, he is a pilot... But he wants to be referred to as the captain.
jokes.setup: Argument about whether to call the pilot 'captain'
jokes.punchline: Reveals pilot has pretentious title preference
jokes.explanation: Minor absurdity about professional titles and ego

jokes.text: Call me Goat Williker. Fine, Goat Williker. The pilot wants your weight.
jokes.setup: Jeff makes point about respecting title preferences
jokes.punchline: Creates ridiculous name to test Larry's commitment to the principle
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation testing social courtesy with absurd made-up name

jokes.text: The Willikers do not give their weight.
jokes.setup: Joins Jeff's ridiculous name game
jokes.punchline: Adopts the silly surname and makes weight secrecy a family policy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the absurd name into family identity

jokes.text: Nothing's wrong with the plane. We're just... heavy.
jokes.setup: Passengers worry about unscheduled landing
jokes.punchline: Captain delivers bad news with underwhelming casualness
jokes.explanation: Good understatement that makes serious aviation issue sound trivial

jokes.text: 'Cause I asked you for your weights, you wouldn't tell me, so I had to guess your weights. And now, now we're over.
jokes.setup: The weight secrecy has consequences
jokes.punchline: Larry's guessing caused the overweight problem
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing consequences of the running weight gag

jokes.text: No. No fucking two out of three. You don't get a second chance, Larry. It's not the damn playoffs.
jokes.setup: Larry loses rock-paper-scissors to decide whose bags stay
jokes.punchline: Leon shuts down Larry's attempt to get a do-over with sports analogy
jokes.explanation: Leon's no-nonsense response to Larry's whining with good sports metaphor

jokes.text: And I don't know why you assholes couldn't give me your weight!
jokes.setup: Larry's luggage gets thrown off the plane
jokes.punchline: Blames everyone else for the weight secrecy that caused the problem
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - takes no responsibility while blaming others for predictable consequences

jokes.text: You've only been here five minutes.
jokes.setup: Larry complains about mosquito bite immediately upon arrival
jokes.punchline: Jeff points out the absurdly quick timeline for complaints
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Larry's immediate negativity upon arrival

jokes.text: I'm still undecided myself.
jokes.setup: Leon asks if Larry is coming to the wedding
jokes.punchline: Larry claims uncertainty about attending a wedding he's already traveled to Mexico for
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - travels internationally but still uncommitted to actually attending

jokes.text: You figured one of us wouldn't come is what you figured. Right?
jokes.setup: Mickey says he figured Larry and Cheryl would work it out
jokes.punchline: Larry sees through the diplomatic lie to Mickey's real expectation
jokes.explanation: Larry cuts through social politeness to uncomfortable truth about divorced couple invitations

jokes.text: What you just witnessed was a whirlwind of smarm.
jokes.setup: Mickey leaves after being overly friendly
jokes.punchline: Larry describes Mickey's behavior with vivid meteorological metaphor
jokes.explanation: Great Larry phrase combining weather imagery with personality criticism

jokes.text: All right, maybe I am trying to get you to say something bad about Mickey. Well, I am not going to say anything bad about Mickey.
jokes.setup: Larry admits his manipulation tactics
jokes.punchline: Jeff doubles down on his Mickey loyalty despite the admission
jokes.explanation: Advances the running gag of Jeff's stubborn loyalty even when Larry admits trying to manipulate him

jokes.text: Hey, you know, I would unpack my bag, but I don't have one.
jokes.setup: Larry enters his hotel room
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to losing luggage on the plane
jokes.explanation: Good callback delivered with typical Larry matter-of-fact frustration

jokes.text: I said 122. Oh. That was the exact right answer.
jokes.setup: Donna asks what Larry guessed for her weight
jokes.punchline: She confirms his guess was exactly right, making the earlier scene more awkward
jokes.explanation: Retroactively makes the weight revelation scene more uncomfortable by confirming accuracy

jokes.text: I want to make a good first-breath impression.
jokes.setup: Larry needs a toothbrush badly
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'first impression' but focuses on breath specifically
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Larry's obsession with social presentation details

jokes.text: Why the snarky attitude toward a fellow Jew? She's Jewish? Yeah. Bat mitzvahed? Big bat mitzvah.
jokes.setup: Jeff calling out Larry's attitude toward Donna
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate interest once he learns she's Jewish
jokes.explanation: Larry's tribal loyalty overriding his weight concerns - shows his priority hierarchy.

jokes.text: Get the fuck out! Go back to your little fucking hovel, with your unbrushed teeth!
jokes.setup: Larry asks to search their room for toothbrush
jokes.punchline: Susie explodes with specific insults about his room and hygiene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie explosion with vivid imagery about Larry's degraded state

jokes.text: I told 'em I was handicapped. If they ask too many questions, then I throw a limp on their ass.
jokes.setup: Leon has a huge hotel room despite not attending the wedding
jokes.punchline: Reveals he faked disability for room upgrade with specific performance detail
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon scheme with specific acting details that make it funnier and more wrong

jokes.text: Do you think the maid is gonna charge me for the massage she gave me? 'Cause she didn't mention it.
jokes.setup: Leon is living it up in his fake handicap room
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions getting sexual services from hotel staff
jokes.explanation: Leon's shameless exploitation of his fake disability extends to inappropriate services

jokes.text: We do have an extra toothbrush. I can't believe it. That's so great! I'm sorry, I misspoke. It's for us.
jokes.setup: Larry finally finds someone with an extra toothbrush
jokes.punchline: They immediately take it back, crushing his hopes
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that builds hope then immediately crushes it for maximum frustration

jokes.text: Mine's a fluke. It's a fluke emergency! A toothbrush emergency, it's one in a million!
jokes.setup: Mickey won't give up emergency toothbrush because Larry having emergency proves they need one
jokes.punchline: Larry argues his emergency is so rare it shouldn't count as precedent
jokes.explanation: Great Larry desperation logic trying to minimize his own emergency to get what he needs

jokes.text: And you know why I know that? Because I have a goddamn extra toothbrush.
jokes.setup: Larry argues they won't have a toothbrush emergency
jokes.punchline: Sasha points out their preparedness prevents the emergency Larry is having
jokes.explanation: Perfect circular logic that shuts down Larry's argument using his own situation as proof

jokes.text: Same mouth, the mouth is the same. Look at the esteem that I hold your mouth in.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to convince Cheryl to let him use her toothbrush
jokes.punchline: Frames sharing germs with ex-wife as a compliment about her mouth
jokes.explanation: Awkward Larry attempt to make sharing toothbrush sound romantic rather than gross

jokes.text: I had a 48-year toothbrushing streak. I couldn't let it fall by the wayside.
jokes.setup: Ted catches Larry using Cheryl's toothbrush in their bathroom
jokes.punchline: Larry explains his desperate behavior with an impressive but ridiculous statistic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obsession with meaningless personal records that drive inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: I used my finger. This does not smell like a minty finger.
jokes.setup: Larry lies about having brushed his teeth
jokes.punchline: Donna's detective work exposes his obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good detective work by Donna catching Larry in an obvious lie through physical evidence

jokes.text: This is not like five M&M's. This is like a cudgel, this thing.
jokes.setup: Larry finds Donna eating large Toblerone chocolate
jokes.punchline: Describes the chocolate bar as a weapon due to its size
jokes.explanation: Good visual description that connects to Larry's fears about her eating habits

jokes.text: You're punishing me for being honest. I didn't have to tell you.
jokes.setup: Hotel charges Larry $250 for reporting the stain
jokes.punchline: Larry identifies the perverse incentive structure
jokes.explanation: Good Larry observation about how honesty gets punished in business dealings

jokes.text: Always a gamble. There's always a possibility of mold. You got to really inspect 'em. You need like a jeweler's loupe.
jokes.setup: Larry takes a raspberry from their breakfast
jokes.punchline: Describes elaborate inspection process for fruit safety
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obsession with worst-case food scenarios requiring professional equipment

jokes.text: I don't bite 'em. Do you bite a raspberry? Sometimes it gets stuck in your teeth. What do you do then?
jokes.setup: Larry continues raspberry analysis
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate raspberry-eating problem that connects to his toothbrush obsession
jokes.explanation: Connects fruit anxiety to dental hygiene concerns in typical Larry fashion

jokes.text: I'm sure you don't want to be considered a... schnorrer.
jokes.setup: Larry pressures Donna to pay for plane
jokes.punchline: Uses Yiddish term for freeloader to manipulate her Jewish guilt
jokes.explanation: Larry using cultural-specific guilt tactics with deliberate Yiddish term

jokes.text: Yeah, my understanding was this was couples, babe. And we're a couple, Larry.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to make Donna pay separately
jokes.punchline: She asserts couple status after one date to avoid paying
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where she manipulates the relationship timeline for financial advantage

jokes.text: I'm not gonna say anything bad about Mickey. I'll tell you that much.
jokes.setup: Larry starts complaining about Mickey again
jokes.punchline: Jeff preemptively shuts down anti-Mickey talk for the third time
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag, building anticipation for eventual payoff

jokes.text: I always carry an extra dress in case there's a dress emergency. It's gonna look gorgeous on you.
jokes.setup: Donna needs clothes since her luggage was lost
jokes.punchline: Susie has prepared for this exact scenario with over-the-top confidence
jokes.explanation: Susie's preparedness contrasts with everyone else's lack of emergency planning

jokes.text: I don't give my weight, or my fuckin' height. That's called a goddamn description of a motherfucker.
jokes.setup: Larry still trying to get weights for return flight
jokes.punchline: Leon treats basic measurements as police lineup information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon paranoia turning innocent measurements into criminal identification

jokes.text: This is better than Mocha Joe's. Mocha Joe can't fuck with this right here.
jokes.setup: They taste amazing coffee at the hotel
jokes.punchline: Realizes they found the beans that can beat their rival
jokes.explanation: Good callback that advances the beans subplot with discovery of superior product

jokes.text: My poor sister is... she's quite ill. She only has a few months to live. And the only pleasure in life that she gets at all is from her morning coffee.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to get secret coffee bean information
jokes.punchline: Invents elaborate dying sister story for coffee beans
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry manipulation using fake tragedy, showing how far he'll go for coffee superiority

jokes.text: Well, she had, uh, Bell's palsy, and then it turned into... Rosenbaum's palsy, which is quite lethal.
jokes.setup: Larry invents sister's illness for beans
jokes.punchline: Creates fake medical condition using Jewish surname
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvisation creating fake disease with ethnic surname twist

jokes.text: All she can do, really, is lift the cup up to her lips and swallow. Literally that is all she can do.
jokes.setup: Larry explains how paralyzed sister can still drink coffee
jokes.punchline: Describes impossibly specific mobility limitations
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical details that show Larry improvising increasingly ridiculous limitations

jokes.text: 163 pounds, señor. Si? Yes! Right on the head! You nailed it!
jokes.setup: Carnival weight guesser takes on Jeff
jokes.punchline: Perfectly guesses Jeff's weight he's been hiding all episode
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to weight secrecy - expert instantly determines what Jeff desperately hid

jokes.text: I would guess the lady weighs 155 pounds, señor.
jokes.setup: Weight guesser volunteers unsolicited guess about Donna
jokes.punchline: Publicly announces the weight of woman already sensitive about weight changes
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed to create maximum awkwardness given Donna's weight sensitivity

jokes.text: Why, you charlatan! You quack! You have besmirched this woman's honor.
jokes.setup: Larry needs to create distraction from accurate weight guess
jokes.punchline: Uses elaborate Victorian language to defend Donna's honor
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Larry's normal speech and sudden chivalrous formality

jokes.text: Ernesto, you stink! Thank you very much. You did a fantastic job. I've got five people coming later. I want you to guess their weights, too.
jokes.setup: Larry insults carnival worker for guessing Donna's weight
jokes.punchline: Immediately praises him and plans to use his services
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction showing Larry's opportunistic nature - insults then immediately recruits the same person

jokes.text: Ernesto. They're here.
jokes.setup: Larry spots his friends at carnival
jokes.punchline: Signals his weight-guesser accomplice that targets have arrived
jokes.explanation: Great setup showing Larry has recruited Ernesto for his weight-gathering scheme

jokes.text: You've been sleeping with Cheryl. You've been doing it for a while, too. Haven't you?
jokes.setup: Larry finds Ted's lens cloth under Cheryl's bed
jokes.punchline: Realizes the affair explains all the suspicious behavior
jokes.explanation: Good detective payoff connecting earlier lens cloth mystery to affair revelation

jokes.text: This is why you don't invite divorced couples to a wedding, Mickey.
jokes.setup: Fight breaks out at the wedding after affair revelation
jokes.punchline: Larry vindicated his earlier theory about divorced couples being combustible
jokes.explanation: Perfect validation callback - Larry's social theory proves exactly right at worst moment

jokes.text: Larry, nobody likes you.
jokes.setup: Wedding is ruined by Larry's revelations
jokes.punchline: Groom delivers brutal assessment of Larry's social standing
jokes.explanation: Harsh but possibly accurate assessment delivered at peak wedding chaos

jokes.text: I have the weights! Because the carnival barker, the best weight-guesser in all of Mexico gave me this.
jokes.setup: Everyone refuses to give weights for return flight
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals his elaborate carnival scheme worked perfectly
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff showing Larry's manipulative planning worked despite social chaos

jokes.text: That's diabolical, Lar.
jokes.setup: Larry's carnival weight scheme is revealed
jokes.punchline: Jeff appreciates Larry's elaborate manipulation despite being a victim
jokes.explanation: Jeff's grudging admiration for Larry's scheming despite being manipulated

jokes.text: No, it's not the passengers. It's the cargo. bags of beans. They're all gonna have to go.
jokes.setup: Plane still overweight despite having passenger weights
jokes.punchline: Leon's stolen coffee beans are the real weight problem
jokes.explanation: Good twist - the weight obsession was pointless, Leon's theft is the real problem
